title: synaphea polypodioides
intro: Synaphea polypodioides, also known as the Western synaphea or holly bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves of this plant have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and fever, due to its health benefits. Synaphea polypodioides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or poultice to utilize its medicinal properties.
